## func [splitChunks](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/coordinator.go#L634>)

```go
func splitChunks(chunks []ChunkMeta, n int) [][]ChunkMeta
```

\* splitChunks \- partitions chunks into groups for parallel execution.

Behaviors: Distributes chunks evenly across N groups and removes empty groups.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Does not consider chunk size imbalance. 2. Ignores data locality.

<a name="Coordinator"></a>
## type [Coordinator](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/coordinator.go#L15-L35>)



```go
type Coordinator struct {
    Addr string

    mu      sync.Mutex
    chunks  map[ChunkID]ChunkMeta
    jobs    map[JobID]JobSpec
    status  map[JobID]JobStatus
    results map[JobID]JobResult

    workers  []string
    jobQueue chan JobSpec
    // failure recovery module
    // wal            *WAL
    // checkpointPath string
    // raft
    rf      *raft.RaftPeer
    applyCh <-chan raft.ApplyMsg
    pending map[int]chan struct{}
    applied map[int]bool
}
```

<a name="NewCoordinator"></a>
### func [NewCoordinator](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/coordinator.go#L52>)

```go
func NewCoordinator(addr string, workers []string, id int, raftPeers []string, ctrlPeers []string) *Coordinator
```

\* NewCoordinator \-\- initializes a Coordinator instance with Raft\-backed metadata replication.

This sets up:

```
in memory metadata state (chunks, jobs, status, results)
Raft peer for replicated state machine
Then apply channel for committed log entries and do
```

Behavior:

```
If raftPeers / ctrlPeers are not provided, defaults are used.
Raft is immediately activated before returning.
```

Limitations / potential failure scenarios:

1. Assumes peer lists are correctly aligned \(same length\); panics otherwise.
2. Does not validate network reachability of peers at initialization.
3. Coordinator state is in\-memory; durability depends entirely on Raft.

<a name="Coordinator.Start"></a>
### func \(\*Coordinator\) [Start](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/coordinator.go#L114>)

```go
func (c *Coordinator) Start() error
```

\* Start \-\- starts background loops and exposes HTTP endpoints for the coordinator.

Behaviors: Launches the Raft apply loop and scheduler loop, then starts an HTTP server providing endpoints for chunk registration, job submission, and result queries.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Blocking call; does not support graceful shutdown. 2. Background goroutines are not terminated if server fails. 3. There are no health checks or restart mechanisms.

<a name="Coordinator.applyDAMBTCommand"></a>
### func \(\*Coordinator\) [applyDAMBTCommand](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/coordinator.go#L759>)

```go
func (c *Coordinator) applyDAMBTCommand(cmd DAMBTCommand)
```

\* applyDAMBTCommand \- applies a committed Raft command to local state.

Behaviors: Updates metadata state \(chunks, jobs, status, results\) according to the operation encoded in the command.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Assumes commands are valid and well\-formed. 2. No schema/version control for command evolution.

<a name="Coordinator.findChunksLocked"></a>
### func \(\*Coordinator\) [findChunksLocked](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/coordinator.go#L427>)

```go
func (c *Coordinator) findChunksLocked(job JobSpec) []ChunkMeta
```

\* findChunksLocked \- finds all chunks overlapping a job query.

Behaviors: Iterates over chunk metadata and returns all chunks matching the instrument and time range of the job.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Must be called with mutex held \(not thread\-safe\). 2. Linear scan; inefficient for large metadata sets. 3. No indexing or caching optimization.

<a name="Coordinator.handleJobResult"></a>
### func \(\*Coordinator\) [handleJobResult](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/coordinator.go#L292>)

```go
func (c *Coordinator) handleJobResult(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request)
```

\* handleJobResult \- retrieves the final result of a completed job.

Behaviors: Returns stored job results if available, or indicates if the job is still running or has not produced output.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Returns 202 if job is not finished. 2. Returns 404 if result is missing after completion. 3. Cannot stream partial or intermediate results.

<a name="Coordinator.handleJobStatus"></a>
### func \(\*Coordinator\) [handleJobStatus](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/coordinator.go#L261>)

```go
func (c *Coordinator) handleJobStatus(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request)
```

\* handleJobStatus \- returns the current status of a job.

Behaviors: Reads replicated state to return the latest job status, including queued, running, completed, or failed states.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Returns 404 if job does not exist. 2. No progress granularity beyond coarse status.

<a name="Coordinator.handleRegisterChunk"></a>
### func \(\*Coordinator\) [handleRegisterChunk](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/coordinator.go#L136>)

```go
func (c *Coordinator) handleRegisterChunk(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request)
```

\* handleRegisterChunk \- registers chunk metadata via a replicated command.

Behaviors: Validates incoming chunk metadata and submits a Raft command to ensure consistent replication of chunk state across coordinators.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Rejects invalid chunk definitions \(missing ID or replicas\). 2. Fails if current node is not Raft leader. 3. No deduplication or version control for repeated registrations.

<a name="Coordinator.handleSubmitJob"></a>
### func \(\*Coordinator\) [handleSubmitJob](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/coordinator.go#L195>)

```go
func (c *Coordinator) handleSubmitJob(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request)
```

\* handleSubmitJob \- submits a new backtesting job into the system.

Behaviors: Validates request parameters, constructs a JobSpec, and submits it through Raft so that job creation is replicated and consistent.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Rejects invalid time ranges or missing fields. 2. Fails if current node is not Raft leader. 3. Does not validate correctness of strategy logic.

<a name="Coordinator.markJobFailed"></a>
### func \(\*Coordinator\) [markJobFailed](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/coordinator.go#L465>)

```go
func (c *Coordinator) markJobFailed(jobID JobID)
```

\* markJobFailed \-\- marks a job as failed via Raft replication.

Behaviors: Submits a job status update command to ensure failure state is consistently replicated across all coordinators.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Assumes job exists in metadata. 2. Relies on Raft commit success.

<a name="Coordinator.raftApplyLoop"></a>
### func \(\*Coordinator\) [raftApplyLoop](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/coordinator.go#L843>)

```go
func (c *Coordinator) raftApplyLoop()
```

\* raftApplyLoop \- processes committed Raft log entries continuously.

Behaviors: Reads from apply channel, decodes commands, applies them to local state, and signals waiting goroutines for completion.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Decode errors are logged but not retried. 2. Silent skip of empty commands, which could be an UB.

<a name="Coordinator.runJobParallel"></a>
### func \(\*Coordinator\) [runJobParallel](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/coordinator.go#L666>)

```go
func (c *Coordinator) runJobParallel(job JobSpec, chunks []ChunkMeta) (JobResult, error)
```

\* runJobParallel \- executes a job across multiple workers concurrently.

Behaviors: Splits chunks into groups, runs them in parallel using goroutines, and merges partial results into a final output.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Fails if any sub\-task fails. 2. No partial recovery or retry at group level. 3. Numerical faults do carry on.

<a name="Coordinator.runJobWithRetry"></a>
### func \(\*Coordinator\) [runJobWithRetry](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/coordinator.go#L604>)

```go
func (c *Coordinator) runJobWithRetry(startIndex int, job JobSpec, chunks []ChunkMeta) (JobResult, int, error)
```

\* runJobWithRetry \- retries job execution across workers.

Behaviors: Attempts execution on multiple workers in sequence until one succeeds or all fail.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Sequential retries increase latency. 2. Returns only last encountered error.

<a name="Coordinator.schedulerLoop"></a>
### func \(\*Coordinator\) [schedulerLoop](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/coordinator.go#L325>)

```go
func (c *Coordinator) schedulerLoop()
```

\* schedulerLoop \- continuously schedules and dispatches jobs to workers.

Behaviors: Consumes queued jobs, ensures leader only execution, updates job status, assigns chunks, and executes jobs in parallel across workers.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Only leader schedules jobs; followers remain idle. 2. No load balancing or prioritization strategy. 3. Jobs fail entirely if worker execution fails.

<a name="Coordinator.submitMetadataCommand"></a>
### func \(\*Coordinator\) [submitMetadataCommand](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/coordinator.go#L804>)

```go
func (c *Coordinator) submitMetadataCommand(cmd DAMBTCommand) error
```

\* submitMetadataCommand \- submits a command through Raft and waits for commit.

Behaviors: Encodes and submits a command to Raft, blocks until the command is applied, and synchronizes using per\-index channels.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Returns error if node is not leader. 2. Fixed timeout may fail under slow consensus.

```go
type DAMBTCommand struct {
    Op     string     `json:"op"`
    Chunk  *ChunkMeta `json:"chunk,omitempty"`

    JobID  JobID      `json:"job_id,omitempty"`
    Status JobStatus  `json:"status,omitempty"`
    Result *JobResult `json:"result,omitempty"`
}
```

```go
}
```

<a name="NewDataNode"></a>
### func [NewDataNode](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/datanode.go#L24>)

```go
func NewDataNode(addr string, dataDir string) *DataNode
```

\* NewDataNode \- initializes a data node instance.

Behaviors: Stores the node address and data directory configuration used by HTTP handlers for chunk storage and retrieval.

<a name="DataNode.Start"></a>
### func \(\*DataNode\) [Start](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/datanode.go#L39>)

```go
func (d *DataNode) Start() error
```

\* Start \- starts the data node HTTP server.

Behaviors: Creates the local data directory and exposes HTTP endpoints for storing and fetching market data chunks.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Fails if the data directory cannot be created. Need os permission.

<a name="DataNode.chunkPath"></a>
### func \(\*DataNode\) [chunkPath](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/datanode.go#L133>)

```go
func (d *DataNode) chunkPath(id ChunkID) string
```



<a name="DataNode.handleGetChunk"></a>
### func \(\*DataNode\) [handleGetChunk](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/datanode.go#L109>)

```go
func (d *DataNode) handleGetChunk(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request)
```

\* handleGetChunk \- retrieves a chunk through the HTTP API.

Behaviors: Validates the query parameter, reads the corresponding CSV chunk file, and returns the market events as JSON.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Rejects non\-GET requests or missing chunk IDs. 2. Returns not found if the chunk file cannot be opened or parsed.

<a name="DataNode.handlePutChunk"></a>
### func \(\*DataNode\) [handlePutChunk](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/datanode.go#L71>)

```go
func (d *DataNode) handlePutChunk(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request)
```

\* handlePutChunk \- stores a chunk through the HTTP API.

Behaviors: Validates the request, decodes market events, writes them to a CSV chunk file, and returns a success response with the stored chunk ID.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Rejects non\-POST requests or invalid JSON bodies. 2. Fails if chunk ID is missing or file writing fails. 3. Does not compute checksum or update persistent manifest, completed by datanode\_manifest.

<a name="DataNodeManifest"></a>
## type [DataNodeManifest](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/datanode_manifest.go#L21-L25>)



```go
type DataNodeManifest struct {
    mu     sync.Mutex
    Path   string                         `json:"-"`
    Chunks map[ChunkID]ChunkManifestEntry `json:"chunks"`
}
```

<a name="LoadDataNodeManifest"></a>
### func [LoadDataNodeManifest](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/datanode_manifest.go#L37>)

```go
func LoadDataNodeManifest(path string) (*DataNodeManifest, error)
```

\* LoadDataNodeManifest \-\- loads a data node manifest from disk.

Behaviors: Reads the manifest JSON file, initializes an empty manifest if the file does not exist, and restores the chunk metadata map for future storage lookup.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Returns an error if the manifest file exists but cannot be read. 2. Returns an error if JSON decoding fails.

<a name="DataNodeManifest.Get"></a>
### func \(\*DataNodeManifest\) [Get](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/datanode_manifest.go#L107>)

```go
func (m *DataNodeManifest) Get(id ChunkID) (ChunkManifestEntry, bool)
```



<a name="DataNodeManifest.Put"></a>
### func \(\*DataNodeManifest\) [Put](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/datanode_manifest.go#L99>)

```go
func (m *DataNodeManifest) Put(e ChunkManifestEntry) error
```



<a name="DataNodeManifest.Save"></a>
### func \(\*DataNodeManifest\) [Save](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/datanode_manifest.go#L78>)

```go
func (m *DataNodeManifest) Save() error
```

\* Save \-\- persists the data node manifest atomically.

Behaviors: Serializes the manifest as indented JSON, writes it to a temporary file, and renames the temporary file into the final manifest path.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Fails if the manifest directory cannot be created. 2. Fails if JSON serialization or file writing fails. 3. Atomicity depends on os.Rename behavior of the underlying filesystem.

```go
}
```

<a name="DataNodeRPCServer"></a>
## type [DataNodeRPCServer](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/datanode_rpc.go#L17-L21>)



```go
type DataNodeRPCServer struct {
    Addr     string
    DataDir  string
    Manifest *DataNodeManifest
}
```

<a name="NewDataNodeRPCServer"></a>
### func [NewDataNodeRPCServer](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/datanode_rpc.go#L40>)

```go
func NewDataNodeRPCServer(addr string, dataDir string) *DataNodeRPCServer
```

\* NewDataNodeRPCServer \- initializes a data node RPC server.

Behaviors: Creates the data directory if needed, loads the persistent chunk manifest, and prepares the server for PutChunk and GetChunk RPC operations.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Panics if the manifest cannot be loaded. 2. Does not validate whether existing manifest entries point to valid files.

<a name="DataNodeRPCServer.GetChunk"></a>
### func \(\*DataNodeRPCServer\) [GetChunk](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/datanode_rpc.go#L112>)

```go
func (d *DataNodeRPCServer) GetChunk(id ChunkID) (GetChunkResponse, remote.RemoteError)
```

\* GetChunk \- retrieves a stored chunk after checksum verification.

Behaviors: Looks up chunk metadata in the manifest, verifies the file checksum, reads events from disk, and returns them to the caller.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Fails if the chunk ID is missing or not registered.

<a name="DataNodeRPCServer.PutChunk"></a>
### func \(\*DataNodeRPCServer\) [PutChunk](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/datanode_rpc.go#L65>)

```go
func (d *DataNodeRPCServer) PutChunk(req PutChunkRequest) (bool, remote.RemoteError)
```

\* PutChunk \- stores a chunk and records it in the data node manifest.

Behaviors: Validates the chunk ID, writes market events to disk, computes a checksum, marks the chunk as sealed, and persists its metadata in the manifest.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Fails if chunk ID is missing or storage directory cannot be created. 2. Fails if writing events, computing checksum, or saving manifest fails.

<a name="JobResult"></a>
## type [JobResult](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/types.go#L40-L45>)



```go
type JobResult struct {
    JobID      JobID    `json:"job_id"`
    EventsRead int      `json:"events_read"`
    PnL        float64  `json:"pnl"`
    Logs       []string `json:"logs"`
}
```

<a name="MergeResults"></a>
### func [MergeResults](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/strategy.go#L52>)

```go
func MergeResults(job JobSpec, parts []JobResult) JobResult
```

\* MergeResults \-\- combines partial worker results into one job result.

Behaviors: Aggregates event counts, sums partial PnL values, and concatenates logs from all worker\-produced results.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Assumes partial results are independent and safe to sum. 2. Does not restore global event ordering across worker partitions.

<a name="RunStrategy"></a>
### func [RunStrategy](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/strategy.go#L14>)

```go
func RunStrategy(job JobSpec, events []MarketEvent) JobResult
```

\* RunStrategy \- executes the requested backtesting strategy over market events.

Behaviors: Sorts events by timestamp, applies the selected strategy logic, and returns a JobResult containing event count, PnL, and execution logs.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Mutates event order in\-place by sorting the input slice. 2. Only implements simple momentum behavior; unknown strategies produce zero PnL. 3. Does not model transaction costs, positions, slippage, or risk constraints.

<a name="runJobOnWorker"></a>
### func [runJobOnWorker](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/coordinator.go#L483>)

```go
func runJobOnWorker(workerAddr string, job JobSpec, chunks []ChunkMeta) (JobResult, error)
```

\* runJobOnWorker \- executes a job on a worker via HTTP.

Behaviors: Sends job and chunk data to a worker endpoint and waits for a synchronous result response.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. No retry logic on failure. 2. Network errors directly propagate.

<a name="runJobOnWorkerRPC"></a>
### func [runJobOnWorkerRPC](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/coordinator.go#L726>)

```go
func runJobOnWorkerRPC(workerAddr string, job JobSpec, chunks []ChunkMeta) (JobResult, error)
```

\* runJobOnWorkerRPC \- executes a job using RPC abstraction.

Behaviors: Dynamically binds RPC client interface and invokes the RunBacktest method on the worker.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. RPC binding may fail due to interface mismatch. 2. Errors are string\-based and not strongly typed.

<a name="WorkerRPCServer.RunBacktest"></a>
### func \(\*WorkerRPCServer\) [RunBacktest](<https://github.com/Yunfan-Wang/DamBackTest/blob/main/dambt/worker_rpc.go#L42>)

```go
func (w *WorkerRPCServer) RunBacktest(req RunJobRequest) (RunJobResponse, remote.RemoteError)
```

\* RunBacktest \- executes a backtesting request on the worker.

Behaviors: Fetches all required chunks, filters events by instrument and time range, runs the configured strategy, and returns the computed job result.

Limitations / potential failure scenarios: 1. Fails if any required chunk cannot be fetched. 2. Loads all events into memory before filtering. 3. Performs no caching, prefetching, or partial retry inside the worker. 4. No advanced features like charting and validating.
